purpose: N-dimensional labeled arrays — used for gridded climate/DEM data

Dataset.note: Collection of DataArrays sharing coordinates. Like a dict of arrays.

gotchas: xarray coordinates may be named 'lat'/'lon' or 'y'/'x' or 'latitude'/'longitude' depending on source. Always check ds.coords.
Spatial mean over a polygon: use .where(mask).mean(dim=['lat','lon']) — not just .mean().
Date selection: .sel(time='2000') selects ALL of year 2000. .sel(time=slice('2000','2001')) is a range.
GridMET output uses 'lat' and 'lon' dims. py3dep output uses 'y' and 'x'.
xarray lazy loads from NetCDF — call .load() or .compute() to force evaluation before looping.
Avoid calling .values on large DataArrays inside loops — vectorize operations instead.
Time coordinates may be cftime objects (not numpy datetime64) for pre-1678 data. Use xr.cftime_range.

common_patterns.spatial_mean_over_time:
# ds has dims (time, lat, lon)
basin_mean = ds['pr'].mean(dim=['lat', 'lon'])  # → DataArray(time)
df = basin_mean.to_pandas()  # → pd.Series
